privileged_info_summary: The task uses real San Francisco parcel and demographic data from the UDST/sanfran_urbansim dataset. By correctly following the instructions to join households to buildings and parcels to obtain zone IDs, and grouping by zone_id, the final dataset should contain exactly 187 qualifying zones (those with at least 10 households). The expected median price-to-income ratio across these zones is approximately 0.00841 (this unusually low value arises because 'residential_sales_price' in this dataset averages ~$543, reflecting price per square foot rather than total home value). The task strictly expects the ratio to be calculated directly from these unscaled values without correction. The verifier dynamically computes these ground truth values from the HDF5 data during setup and explicitly asserts that the agent's output CSV yields a matching zone count (within 20%) and median ratio (within 30%).
